title: Cryptographic Abuse Pattern Detector
reason: You don't have access to the data you need. Harassment complaints are siloed in HR systems and confidential settlements. Even if you did have access, differential privacy would anonymize the abusers, making your tool useless for enforcement. You're trying to solve a data access problem with cryptography.

title: Legislative Impact Simulator
reason: Legislators don't make decisions based on data. They make decisions based on ideology, constituent pressure, and lobbyist money. You're selling a tool to a market that doesn't want it. Even if they wanted it, the problem is unsolvable—legislative impact is fundamentally unpredictable. Any model you build will be wrong, and legislators will ignore it.

title: Peer-to-Peer Lån Gennem Lokale Sociale Netværk
reason: Hvis låntager ikke betaler tilbage, ødelægger det venskabet og långiver mister penge uden sikkerhed. Regulering som bank er dyrt og komplekst. Danske banker er allerede villige til at give små lån.

title: Flying Rabbit Veterinary Pathology Atlas
reason: Flying rabbits do not exist and no credible engineering pathway to create them has been demonstrated; building a diagnostic atlas for a non-existent species is solving a problem that may never materialize, wasting resources on a solution with zero addressable market today and speculative market 20+ years from now.

title: Reverse-Osmosis Lung Membrane Scaffolding
reason: Dissolved oxygen in water (8ppm) is 2,600x lower than oxygen in air (21%); extracting enough O₂ for human metabolism requires membrane area and perfusion rates that would cause fatal pulmonary edema—this is not an engineering problem, it's a physics wall that no scaffold design can overcome.
